validation_rules: build the public Lake project with an explicit named library target
record declaration/import/hash analyzer metadata without proof bodies
execute bounded public certificate transition candidates in a temporary workspace
reject transition candidates with proof bodies, raw tactic scripts, provider bodies, or oracle templates
translate CP2 residual requests into typed action candidates only
accept bounded Evolve policy mutations only with rerun evidence
reject oracle/provider visibility in forward transition rows
reject proof bodies, private source refs, provider payloads, and arbitrary source mutation
receipt_expectations: receipt must cite certificate ids, source refs, source pattern ids, projection receipts, public runtime refs, transition trace, CP2 translation trace, Evolve mutation trace, authority counters, receipt transparency contract, secret-exclusion scan, negative-case coverage, and anti-claim
requirements: Build the public Lake project with an explicit named library target.
Keep generated certificate rows separate from the abstract certificate kernel.
Include more than one public certificate family so the fixture is not a single-shape arithmetic witness.
Record declaration/import/hash analyzer metadata without proof bodies.
Route provider/NIM material through typed CP2 action candidates only.
Limit Evolve to certificate routing policy artifacts and require rerun evidence.
Reject oracle/provider visibility in forward transition rows.
Reject proof bodies, raw tactic scripts, private source refs, and arbitrary source mutation.

receipt_transparency_contract.rule: receipts are public evidence; only proof/provider/oracle/private-source/stdout payload bodies are omitted

anti_claim: This standard governs a source-available public certificate-kernel fixture and verifier-lab execution receipt. It does not import macro proof bodies, certify general theorem correctness, call providers, authorize release, or claim benchmark solve-rate.

standard_payload.ontology: A public certificate-kernel execution lab that carries Lean/Lake tool-witness metadata, analyzer receipts, typed CP2 action translation, bounded Evolve policy reruns, negative cases, and authority ceilings without exporting proof, provider, oracle, private-source, stdout, stderr, credential, or account-bound payload bodies.
standard_payload.teleology: Let a cold reader rerun proof-adjacent Microcosm substrate locally while seeing exactly where execution evidence stops and proof correctness, provider authority, and release authority do not begin.
standard_payload.anti_purpose: Treating the public certificate fixture, Lean/Lake return code, analyzer metadata, provider rows, oracle sidecars, or Evolve reruns as general theorem proof authority or benchmark solve-rate authority.
failure_modes: Lean/Lake return code is treated as general proof correctness instead of bounded fixture witness
compiled declaration count is reported without source hash, import, and manifest coupling
proof body or raw tactic script is exported in a public packet or receipt
provider text, oracle ideal answer, or oracle-needed premise id is counted as forward success
CP2 residual request is emitted as an untyped provider instruction instead of a typed action candidate
Evolve mutation changes arbitrary source or omits rerun evidence
private source path, stdout/stderr body, credential, or account-bound payload leaks into public receipt
certificate fixture is promoted to benchmark solve-rate, release, or general theorem authority
standard_payload.rebuild_action: rerun certificate kernel execution lab, proof-lab, status card, and workingness map before widening any proof-adjacent claim

omitted: Lean proof bodies in JSON receipts
omission_receipt.reason: The public Microcosm lab transfers the certificate-kernel pattern and receipt discipline, not private theorem bodies.
